name: Azure Container Apps
description: Microsoft's serverless container platform — Consumption plan bills per-second on vCPU + GB allocated. Useful as a pay-per-use builder or runtime when already on Azure. Authenticates against the Azure Resource Manager (ARM) with a bearer token from Azure AD (service principal client_credentials grant or managed identity).

credential_fields.name: access_token
credential_fields.label: ARM Access Token
credential_fields.description: Azure AD access token with audience=https://management.azure.com/. Get with: az account get-access-token --resource https://management.azure.com/ --query accessToken -o tsv

credential_fields.name: resource_group
credential_fields.label: Resource Group
credential_fields.description: Default resource group for container app operations

tools.name: list_environments
tools.description: List Container App Environments in the resource group.

tools.name: list_apps
tools.description: List container apps in the resource group.

tools.name: create_app
tools.description: Create or update a container app (Consumption plan). Args: app_name, location, environment_id (full ARM id), image, cpu? (0.25..2 cores), memory? (0.5Gi..4Gi), env? (array of {name, value}), ingress_target_port?.

tools.name: get_app
tools.description: Get a container app. Args: app_name.

tools.name: delete_app
tools.description: Delete a container app. Args: app_name.

tools.name: list_revisions
tools.description: List revisions for a container app (each PUT creates a new revision). Args: app_name.

tools.name: create_job
tools.description: Create a one-shot Container Apps Job (per-second billed, terminates on exit). Useful for builds. Args: job_name, location, environment_id, image, command? (array), cpu?, memory?.

tools.name: start_job
tools.description: Start a job execution. Args: job_name.
